name: Fast Auto Loans, Inc.
description_short: Arizona direct lender offering vehicle title loans ($300–$15,000) and registration loans up to $2,500 with same-day cash and all credit types accepted.
description_long:
Fast Auto Loans, Inc. is a direct local lender operating multiple brick-and-mortar stores across the Phoenix metro area, with locations in Glendale, Peoria, and two Phoenix locations. The company specializes exclusively in vehicle-secured lending products for Arizona residents, positioning itself as a community-facing alternative to national online lenders. It is not a broker — applications submitted online are routed directly to the nearest local store for processing.

The company offers two core products. Title loans range from $300 to $15,000 and require the borrower to hold a lien-free vehicle title, which is used as collateral. Registration loans go up to $2,500 and are available to borrowers who cannot provide a lien-free title but do have a current Arizona vehicle registration, proof of income, and a checking account. Both products can be initiated online or in person, and the company advertises same-day funding with a process that can take as little as 30 minutes from application to cash.

What distinguishes Fast Auto Loans from national title lending chains is its local direct-lending model. Store representatives call applicants directly, explain terms, and verify required documents before the customer visits. The company also serves Spanish-speaking customers (Español option on the website) and explicitly welcomes all credit types, including bad credit, making credit history a secondary factor in approval decisions behind vehicle condition and documentation.

Honestly assessed, title loans are among the most expensive forms of consumer borrowing, and Fast Auto Loans does not display APRs or fee schedules on its location pages — these are deferred to a separate Rates, Fees & Terms page. The vehicle serves as collateral, meaning default carries the real risk of repossession. Registration loans are less risky in that respect but are capped at $2,500. The product is useful for genuine emergencies when no lower-cost option is available, but borrowers should treat this as a last resort and understand the full cost of borrowing before signing.
pros: Title loans range from $300 to $15,000, covering a wider range than most payday or small-dollar lenders
Registration loans up to $2,500 available without requiring a lien-free title
Same-day cash disbursement possible — company advertises approval and funding in as little as 30 minutes
All credit types accepted, including bad credit — approval based more on vehicle and documents than credit score
Multiple physical locations across Phoenix metro (Glendale, Peoria, two Phoenix locations)
Hybrid model: apply online or in person, whichever is more convenient
Spanish language service available (Español) for non-English-speaking borrowers
Direct local lender — online applications go to the nearest store, not a third-party broker network
cons: Title loans require a lien-free vehicle title as collateral — default risks repossession of your vehicle
APRs and specific fee structures are not disclosed on location pages; borrowers must seek out the Rates, Fees & Terms page before committing
Service area is limited to Arizona only — not available to out-of-state borrowers
Registration loans are capped at $2,500, limiting usefulness for larger cash needs
Title lending as a product category typically carries very high effective interest rates relative to personal loans or credit unions
best_for: Arizona residents who own a vehicle outright and need fast emergency cash between $300 and $15,000
Borrowers with poor or no credit history who do not qualify for personal loans or credit union products
People who need same-day funding for an unexpected expense and cannot wait for bank processing
Arizona vehicle owners who lack a lien-free title but have current registration, income, and a checking account (registration loan option)
diagnosis: Fast Auto Loans, Inc. is best suited for Arizona residents facing a genuine financial emergency who own a vehicle and have exhausted lower-cost options. The main caveat is that title loans are secured by your vehicle — missing payments can result in repossession — and the company does not prominently display APRs, so borrowers must review the Rates, Fees & Terms page carefully before signing.
services: Phone callback from local store representative
